one_time_description: Husky Oil spill litigation loan and related expenses (professional fees $462,561 + insurance $420,099) totaling $882,660 expensed; $932,263 loan drawn to fund litigation costs
economic_notes: First Nation is a member of Battleford Agency Tribal Chiefs (BATC) entities including BATC Investments Limited Partnership (20% interest) and BATC Land Holdings Limited Partnership (16.67% interest). COVID-19 impacted operations including casino closures reducing BATC CDC funding. Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. consolidated. Sweetgrass Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and Sweetgrass Legacy Trust not consolidated as Chief and Council do not control the Trusts. Litigation filed against Husky Oil for oil spill on Sweetgrass land.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2021. Province is Saskatchewan (auditor signed in Saskatoon, SK; Treaty 6 Territory). Consolidated entities include Sweetgrass First Nation, Sweetgrass First Nation C.M.H.C. Housing, Sweetgrass Developments Limited Partnership, and Sweetgrass Investments Ltd. Sweetgrass Treaty Land Entitlement Trust and Sweetgrass Legacy Trust excluded from consolidation. Emphasis of Matter paragraph in auditor report regarding litigation loan against Husky Oil for oil spill ($932,263 drawn). All deferred revenue relates to ISC funding. CMHC Section 95 Phase 11 and 12 advances of $346,949 classified as term loan subject to refinancing. Segment schedules were partially cut off (Other, Ottawa Trust Fund, Own Source, Health segments not fully provided in text). Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($8,164,489) plus CMHC ($324,417) = $8,488,906. OSR calculated as $10,829,578 - $8,488,906 = $2,340,672. Chief and Council remuneration schedule not provided in extracted text.
